    "summary": "Quote for 2.5-ton Carrier 16 SEER2 heat pump system replacement in Charlotte, NC at $9,200 total. System includes indoor air handler (FV4CNF002) and outdoor condenser (25HCC624A003) with matched AHRI rating, plus 80% AFUE furnace, 1-day labor, and permit. RED FLAGS: R-410A refrigerant in new equipment may violate EPA AIM Act provisions effective 2025-2026; verify pre-2025 inventory status. Furnace efficiency (80% AFUE) is obsolete by current standards. Only 1-year warranty on parts/labor is notably brief. No load calculation or detailed labor breakdown provided.   "summary_for_llm": "Total: $9,200.\nSystem: heat_pump.\nBrand: Carrier.\nSEER: 16.\nAFUE: 80%.\nTonnage: 2.5 tons.\nRefrigerant: R-410A.\nParts warranty: 1 year.\nLabor warranty: 1 year workmanship.\n\n5 red flag(s):\n  - R-410A refrigerant specified for new equipment installation. Per EPA AIM Act Technology Transitions rule, manufacture/import of new R-410A residential AC and heat pump equipment was prohibited as of Jan 1, 2025. Verify equipment is pre-2025 manufactured inventory and that warranty/service support is available.\n  - Furnace efficiency at 80% AFUE is significantly below current federal minimum (92-96% AFUE for new gas furnaces).\n  - No Manual J load calculation mentioned for full system replacement.\n  - Labor cost breakdown not provided; cannot verify if $9,200 allocation is reasonable.\n  - Warranty period of only 1 year on parts and labor is below industry standard (typically 5-10 years parts, 2-5 years labor on quality installs).\n\nSummary: Quote for 2.5-ton Carrier 16 SEER2 heat pump system replacement in Charlotte, NC at $9,200 total. System includes indoor air handler (FV4CNF002) and outdoor condenser (25HCC624A003) with matched AHRI rating, plus 80% AFUE furnace, 1-day labor, and permit. RED FLAGS: R-410A refrigerant in new equipment may violate EPA AIM Act provisions effective 2025-2026; verify pre-2025 inventory status. Furnace efficiency (80% AFUE) is obsolete by current standards. Only 1-year warranty on parts/labor is notably brief. No load calculation or detailed labor breakdown provided. Overall price appears mid-range for a full system replacement, but compliance and warranty concerns require clarification before proceeding."
